title: [$250] Add a quick filter for workspace merchant rules

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
Similar to [functionality and logic](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/81803) recently added for approval workflows, let's add a quick search filter for merchant rules. **Design and product details:** - The quick filter appears when a user more than 3 distinct merchant rules - The search input search the merchant corresponding tot to the merchant rule - We'll use "contains" logic for the search query

Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com/ 2. Sign up with a Gmail address with a + 3. Choose "Manage my team's expenses" and select QBD (complete the other options with any selection) 4. Open console and enter the following snippet (edit to be the actual date): Onyx.merge('nvp_private_lastDayFreeTrial', '2026-3-7 01:01:00'); 5. Run the following snippet: Onyx.merge('nvp_private_amountOwed', 1000); 6. Run the following snippet: Onyx.merge('nvp_private_billingGracePeriodEnd', 1719792000); 7. Click FAB > Create manual expense > Enter any amount > Select the Workspace ## Expected Result: When creating an expense with ended free trial, the payment paywall should be displayed. ## Actual Result: When creating an expense, the payment paywall appears and disappears briefly. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb90d31c-69b0-4618-9545-f79eb45e48f8 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Android app crashes with UnsupportedOperationException in IntentHandlerFactory.getIntentHandler

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Description The Android app crashes when another app shares content to Expensify using an unrecognized MIME type (e.g. `*/*`). The crash is a fatal `UnsupportedOperationException` originating from `IntentHandlerFactory.getIntentHandler`. This was identified by @linhvovan29546 from Sentry and shared [here](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1772804922204509) in Slack. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. From another Android app, share content to Expensify using an intent with a wildcard MIME type (`*/*`) 2. The app crashes immediately ## Expected Behavior The app should handle unrecognized MIME types gracefully without crashing. ## Actual Behavior Fatal crash with `UnsupportedOperationException("Unsupported MIME type: $mimeType")`. ## Location https://github.com/Expensify/Mobile-Expensify/blob/main/Android/src/yapl/android/managers/intenthandler/IntentHandlerFactory.kt#L13
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @linhvovan29546

  • Upwork Job ID: 2029977842137374960
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-03-06

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked) **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @Leeckhart **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#expensify_bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1772791991222529) ## Action Performed: 1. Corporate card (Chase via [oauth.chase.com](http://oauth.chase.com/)) became disconnected. 2. Card was reconnected (exact disconnect timing unknown). 3. After reconnect, historical transactions were re-imported. 4. Identical transactions were imported more than once for the same card. 5. Users identified and manually deleted some duplicates. ## Expected Result: Reconnecting a Chase card feed should not re-import identical historical transactions. Duplicate transactions with matching cardID, amount, date, and bank source should be automatically deduplicated. ## Actual Result: After reconnecting the Chase card, identical transactions were re-imported, creating duplicate expenses for the same card and transaction details. Users must manually identify and delete duplicates. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos Check the [OP ](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1772791991222529) for details [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @DylanDylann
